
```{r}
set.seed(1)
pacman::p_load(cmdstanr, tidyverse, cowplot, bayesplot)
theme_set(theme_cowplot())
```



```{r}
agent.WBA <- Vectorize(function(
    alpha_prior, beta_prior,
    own_rating, external_rating,
    own_weighting, external_weighting
) {
    alpha_post <- alpha_prior + own_weighting * (own_rating - 1) + external_weighting * (external_rating - 1)
    beta_post  <- beta_prior  + own_weighting * (8 - own_rating) + external_weighting * (8 - external_rating)
    
    alpha_post <- max(alpha_post, 1e-6)  ### Fix dodgy vals
    beta_post  <- max(beta_post,  1e-6)
    
    1 + sum(rbinom(1, size = 7, prob = rbeta(1, alpha_post, beta_post)))
})

agent.PBA <- Vectorize(function(
    alpha_prior, beta_prior,
    own_rating, external_rating,
    own_weighting, external_weighting
) {
    alpha_post <- alpha_prior + own_weighting * (own_rating - 1) + (1 - own_weighting) * (external_rating - 1)
    beta_post  <- beta_prior  + own_weighting * (8 - own_rating) + (1 - own_weighting) * (8 - external_rating)
    
    alpha_post <- max(alpha_post, 1e-6)
    beta_post  <- max(beta_post,  1e-6)
    
    1 + sum(rbinom(1, size = 7, prob = rbeta(1, alpha_post, beta_post)))
})


simulate <- function(
    agent_evaluator,
    n_observations,
    alpha_prior,
    beta_prior,
    own_rating,
    external_rating,
    own_weighting,
    external_weighting
) {
  tibble(
    FaceID=seq(1, n_observations),
    FirstRating=round(runif(n_observations, 1, 8)),
    GroupRating=round(runif(n_observations, 1, 8)),
  ) |> mutate(
    SecondRating=agent_evaluator(
        alpha_prior=alpha_prior,
        beta_prior=beta_prior,
        own_rating=FirstRating,
        external_rating=GroupRating,
        own_weighting=own_weighting,
        external_weighting=external_weighting
    ),
  )
}

simulation <- simulate(
  agent_evaluator=agent.PBA,
  n_observations=100,
  alpha_prior=4,
  beta_prior=3,
  own_weighting=0.5,
  external_weighting=NA
) |> mutate(
    Feedback=GroupRating-FirstRating,
    Change=SecondRating-FirstRating,
    AbsChange=abs(SecondRating-FirstRating)
)

simulation
```


```{r}
ggplot(simulation) +
  geom_jitter(aes(x=Feedback, y=Change), width=0.2, height=0.2, shape=1)
```


```{r fig.width = 10}
simulation |> head(20) |>  ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x=FaceID, y=FirstRating, yend=SecondRating), 
    arrow=arrow(type="closed", length = unit(0.4, "cm"))
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(x=FaceID, y=FirstRating), 
    color="black"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(x=FaceID, y=GroupRating),
    color="red"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(1, 8)) +
  labs(y="Rating")
```





```{r}
simulation |> pivot_longer(
  cols=c(FirstRating, GroupRating, SecondRating),
  names_to="Observation",
  values_to="Rating",
) |> ggplot(aes(x=Rating)) +
  geom_bar() +
  stat_summary(aes(xintercept = after_stat(x), y = 0), fun = mean, geom = "vline", orientation = "y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(1, 8)) +
  facet_wrap(~Observation) +
  labs(y="Observation count")

simulation |> pivot_longer(
  cols=c(Feedback, Change, AbsChange),
  names_to="Observation",
  values_to="Offset",
) |> ggplot(aes(x=Offset)) +
  geom_bar() +
  stat_summary(aes(xintercept = after_stat(x), y = 0), fun = mean, geom = "vline", orientation = "y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(-7, 7), limits=c(-7, 7)) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(Observation, c("Feedback", "Change", "AbsChange"))) +
  labs(y="Observation count")
```

```{r}

## Setting up agent types

agents_wba <- tibble(
  AgentType    = c("Self-Focused", "Balanced", "Socially-Influenced"),
  own_weighting = c(1.5, 1.0, 0.5),
  external_weighting = c(0.5, 1.0, 2.0)
)

agents_pba <- tibble(
  AgentType    = c("Self-Focused", "Balanced", "Socially-Influenced"),
  own_weighting = c(1.5, 1.0, 0.5),
  external_weighting = NA
)

## Global params 
n_observations <- 100
alpha_prior    <- 4
beta_prior     <- 3

## adding columns to identify agent type and ModelType in the results
run_sim <- function(agent_evaluator, own_weighting, external_weighting, AgentType, ModelType) {
  simulate(
    agent_evaluator    = agent_evaluator,
    n_observations     = n_observations,
    alpha_prior        = alpha_prior,
    beta_prior         = beta_prior,
    own_weighting      = own_weighting,
    external_weighting = external_weighting
  ) |> mutate(
    Feedback   = GroupRating - FirstRating,
    Change     = SecondRating - FirstRating,
    AbsChange  = abs(SecondRating - FirstRating),
    AgentType = AgentType,
    ModelType = ModelType
  )
}

## run for pba typologies
results_pba <- agents_pba |>
  pmap(function(AgentType, own_weighting, external_weighting) {
    run_sim(agent.PBA, own_weighting, external_weighting, AgentType, ModelType = "PBA")
  }) |>
  list_rbind() |> drop_na(SecondRating)

## run for wba typologies
results_wba <- agents_wba |>
  pmap(function(AgentType, own_weighting, external_weighting) {
    run_sim(agent.WBA, own_weighting, external_weighting, AgentType, ModelType = "WBA")
  }) |>
  list_rbind()|> drop_na(SecondRating)

## combine
results_all <- bind_rows(results_pba, results_wba)

```


```{r}

## Plot the combined results for each ModelType and AgentType

for (m in unique(results_all$ModelType)) {
  p <- results_all |>
    filter(ModelType == m) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = SecondRating, fill = AgentType)) +
    geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, position = "dodge", colour = "white") +
    facet_wrap(~ AgentType) +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
    labs(title    = paste("Distribution of Second Ratings:", m),
      x = "Second Rating",
      y = "Count",
      fill = "Agent Type") +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  
  print(p)
}

```

```{r}

## Model recovery across PBA and WBA agent typologies
n_sims <- 10 ## EDIT FOR REAL RECOV PLEASE MATTIS

## Function for prepping the data 

prepare_stan_data <- function(df) {
  list(
    N            = nrow(df),
    FirstRating  = as.integer(df$FirstRating),
    GroupRating  = as.integer(df$GroupRating),
    SecondRating = as.integer(df$SecondRating)
  )
}


regenerate_simulations <- TRUE  # set to FALSE to load from disk next time

if (regenerate_simulations || !file.exists("./model_recovery.rds")) {

  ## Build simulation grid 
  ## Cross each agent typology with n_sims replicates
  sim_grid <- bind_rows(
    agents_pba |> mutate(ModelType = "PBA"),
    agents_wba |> mutate(ModelType = "WBA")
  ) |>
    crossing(sim_id = 1:n_sims) 

  ## Fit both candidate models to every simulated dataset
  results_list <- vector("list", nrow(sim_grid))

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(sim_grid))) {
    row             <- sim_grid[i, ]
    true_mod        <- row$ModelType
    true_agent_type <- row$AgentType
    true_own        <- row$own_weighting
    true_ext        <- row$external_weighting  # NA for PBA rows
    id              <- row$sim_id

    ## Simulate fresh data 
    agent_fn <- if (true_mod == "PBA") agent.PBA else agent.WBA

    df <- run_sim(
      agent_evaluator    = agent_fn,
      own_weighting      = true_own,
      external_weighting = true_ext,
      AgentType          = true_agent_type,
      ModelType          = true_mod
    )

    stan_data <- prepare_stan_data(df)
    
    ## Init models 
    
    mod_proportional <- cmdstan_model("./stan/PBA.stan")
    mod_weighted     <- cmdstan_model("./stan/WBA.stan")
    

    ## Fit PBA candidate model
    fit_p <- mod_proportional$sample(
      data          = stan_data,
      chains        = 2,
      iter_warmup   = 500,
      iter_sampling = 500,
      refresh       = 0,
      show_messages = FALSE,
      save_warmup   = TRUE,
      output_dir    = "./temp"
    )

    ## Fit WBA candidate model
    fit_w <- mod_weighted$sample(
      data          = stan_data,
      chains        = 2,
      iter_warmup   = 500,
      iter_sampling = 500,
      refresh       = 0,
      show_messages = FALSE,
      save_warmup   = TRUE,
      output_dir    = "./temp"
    )

    ## LOO-CV for model comparison
    loo_p <- fit_p$loo()
    loo_w <- fit_w$loo()

    weights <- loo::loo_model_weights(
      list(PBA = loo_p, WBA = loo_w),
      method = "stacking"
    )

    ## Posterior draws
    pba_draws <- fit_p$draws(format = "df")
    wba_draws <- fit_w$draws(format = "df")

    results_list[[i]] <- tibble(
      sim_id             = id,
      true_model         = true_mod,
      true_agent_type    = true_agent_type,
      true_own_weighting = true_own,
      true_ext_weighting = true_ext,          # NA for PBA-generated rows

      ## Stacking weights — primary recovery signal
      weight_PBA = as.numeric(weights["PBA"]),
      weight_WBA = as.numeric(weights["WBA"]),

      ## Recovered parameters from PBA candidate
      ## PBA only has own_weighting (external_weighting = 1 - own_weighting)
      pba_recovered_own = mean(pba_draws$p),
      pba_recovered_ext = 1 - mean(pba_draws$p),

      ## Recovered parameters from WBA candidate
      wba_recovered_own = mean(wba_draws$own_weighting),
      wba_recovered_ext = mean(wba_draws$external_weighting)
    )
  }

  recovery_results <- bind_rows(results_list)
  saveRDS(recovery_results, "./model_recovery.rds")

} else {
  recovery_results <- readRDS("./model_recovery.rds")
}

```


```{r}
## Visualizing the sampling process 

# fit wba

np_w <- nuts_params(fit_w)

# Trace plots
bayesplot::mcmc_trace(
  fit_w$draws(format = "array"),
  pars = c("rho", "kappa"),
  np   = np_w
)


# fit pba 
np_p <- nuts_params(fit_p)


# Trace plots
bayesplot::mcmc_trace(
  fit_p$draws(format = "array"),
  pars = "p",
  np   = np_p
)

cat("PBA divergences:", mean(np_p$Value[np_p$Parameter == "divergent__"]) * 100, "%\n")
cat("WBA divergences:", mean(np_w$Value[np_w$Parameter == "divergent__"]) * 100, "%\n")

```




```{r}

## Visualizations of the model recovery - Riccardos Viz - Currently the models are not very distinguishable, its all just PBA

binary_matrix <- recovery_results |>
  # 1. Reshape to make finding the maximum weight per simulation easier
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("weight_"), 
               names_to = "predicted_model", values_to = "weight") |>
  mutate(predicted_model = str_remove(predicted_model, "weight_")) |>
  
  # 2. Keep only the winning model for each simulation
  group_by(sim_id) |>
  slice_max(weight, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup() |>
  
  # 3. Count the wins and calculate proportions per true model
  count(true_model, predicted_model) |>
  group_by(true_model) |>
  mutate(proportion = n / sum(n)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  
  # 4. Ensure we have all combinations factored correctly (handles 0% scenarios)
  mutate(
    true_model = factor(true_model, levels = c("PBA", "WBA")),
    predicted_model = factor(predicted_model, levels = c("PBA", "WBA"))
  ) |>
  complete(true_model, predicted_model, fill = list(n = 0, proportion = 0))


binary_matrix |>
  ggplot(aes(x = predicted_model, y = true_model, fill = proportion)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(proportion, accuracy = 1)),
            size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", direction = -1, limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) + # Keeps SBA at the top left
  labs(
    title = "Model Recovery: Binary Classification",
    x = "Winning Candidate Model (Max Weight)", 
    y = "True Generating Model",
    fill = "Win Rate"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())




```



```{r}
model <- cmdstan_model("./stan/PBA.stan", cpp_options=list(stan_threads=FALSE))

model_fits <- list()

for (type in unique(results_pba$AgentType)) {
  agent_observations <- subset(results_pba, AgentType == type)
  
  model_fit <- model$sample(
    data = list(
      N=nrow(agent_observations),
      FirstRating=agent_observations$FirstRating,
      GroupRating=agent_observations$GroupRating,
      SecondRating=agent_observations$SecondRating
    ),
    seed = 0,
    chains = 4,
    iter_sampling = 1000,
    iter_warmup = 1000,
    refresh = 0
  )
  model_fits[[type]] <- model_fit 
}

```

```{r}
model_fits[["Balanced"]]
```


```{r}

## Testing on real data 

cogsci_pre <- read_csv("./data/cogsci_clean.csv")


## Remove rows in FirstRating, GroupRating and SecondRating that are = 0 

cogsci_pre <- cogsci_pre |> filter(FirstRating != 0, GroupRating != 0, SecondRating != 0)


## Running the model on experimental data 

model <- cmdstan_model("./stan/PBA.stan", cpp_options=list(stan_threads=FALSE)) ## PBA

model_fit <- model$sample(
  data = list(
    N=nrow(cogsci_pre),
    FirstRating=cogsci_pre$FirstRating,
    GroupRating=cogsci_pre$GroupRating,
    SecondRating=cogsci_pre$SecondRating
  ),
  seed = 0,
  chains = 4,
  iter_sampling = 1000,
  iter_warmup = 1000,
  refresh = 0
)


```

```{r}

model_fit$print(max_rows = 30)


```
